summaries.162231: Comments confirm the bug is isolated to Chrome (desktop and Chrome-based browsers like Arc); Safari works fine on web, and Android, iOS, and macOS are unaffected. A commenter identified the root cause: the selection cursor jumps when the height of EditableText exceeds the text height — a threshold confirmed at ~3004px. The issue is also reproducible with SelectableText and linked to similar reports #162698 and #163607. No fix or workaround beyond using Safari is proposed; status is open and stalled.
summaries.120613: Comments debate whether delegating text rendering to platform views is too large a departure from Flutter's pixel-perfect rendering philosophy. The team clarified the plan is not to drop TextField but to integrate platform views inside the current system, letting the browser handle content rendering, selection, carets, and context menus while the framework handles surrounding layout. Rich text editor maintainers (super_editor, Fleather) note their projects use only TextInputConnection so they would not be broken, but concern remains about cases needing custom rendering. A formal design proposal was published at flutter.dev/go/web-native-text-editing. The assignee was removed after a long period with no updates, and a community member noted multiline textfields remain unusable on web iOS and Android.
summaries.143729: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable (3.19.3) and master. The root cause is that FormField stores the validator's return value as a string and does not re-run validators when locale dependencies change; only calling formKey.currentState.validate() again refreshes the error text. A commenter proposed fixing this by re-running validators when dependencies change and adding a BuildContext parameter to FormFieldValidator. A community workaround was shared: a custom BaseForm widget that watches for locale changes via didUpdateWidget and calls validate() in a post-frame callback. No official fix has landed; status is open.
summaries.150048: Root cause identified in comments: ScrollUpdateNotification.dragDetails is null for non-drag scroll events (mouse wheel, trackpad) on desktop/web, so the onDrag dismissal condition is never met. Confirmed affects web and macOS but works correctly on Android (where touch drag events do set dragDetails). PR #154675 fixed the issue but was reverted in PR #161277, so the issue is currently landed-but-reopened. A workaround using a ScrollController listener to manually unfocus on scroll was provided.
summaries.77895: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on macOS desktop but works fine on Flutter web and is still present on stable (3.7.x) and master (3.11.x). The root cause is that arrow key navigation at the edges of the custom widget traverses the global focus scope and moves focus to nearby interactive widgets (e.g., AppBar buttons) rather than staying within the widget. A workaround using FocusScope with onKey returning KeyEventResult.handled prevents traversal out of the scope, but at the cost of disabling Tab navigation out of the scope. Status is open and stalled.
summaries.67365: Comments confirm the floating label of OutlineInputBorder is clipped when the TextField is placed at the very top of its container with no padding above it. A triager initially could not reproduce but was corrected by a commenter who clarified the issue occurs specifically with the first field when there is no top padding. Additionally, a commenter reported the same clipping occurs when wrapping an OutlineInputBorder field in a SizeTransition animation, while UnderlineInputBorder is not affected. Status is open and stalled.
summaries.179482: Root cause identified: iOS 26's semi-transparent keyboard means Flutter still renders a black (or modal-barrier-colored) background under the keyboard instead of showing underlying content, because widgets like Scaffold and BottomSheet move up to avoid the keyboard inset rather than extending below it. Affects iOS 26 only; iOS 18 is fine. The fix requires changes to multiple widgets (Scaffold.resizeToAvoidBottomInset, CupertinoPageScaffold, CupertinoTabScaffold, SearchAnchor, BottomSheet) and is considered a dedicated project, not a hotfix. Workaround: set UIDesignRequiresCompatibility = true in Info.plist (temporary), or avoid mismatched transparent/opaque colors on the modal and its host.
summaries.88354: Comments confirm the keyboard flicker on iOS when switching between obscureText fields is tied to iOS's autofill password bar above the keyboard, which resizes when focus moves between password fields. The behavior is confirmed in native SwiftUI apps as well, suggesting it is an iOS-level behavior rather than a pure Flutter bug. One commenter found the flicker disappears when setting autocorrect: false, while another noted it worsened with iOS 17 and autofillHints. A workaround of autofillHints: [AutofillHints.oneTimeCode] prevents iOS from showing the password bar but breaks password autofill. Reproduced on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.x); no fix has landed.
summaries.109197: Root cause identified in comments: the Android InputMethodManager rejects showSoftInput() calls because the InputConnection between the FlutterView and the current IME is not established when autofocus fires (which happens in a post-frame callback). This is specific to add_to_app; standard Flutter apps work in debug mode. The issue may be related to #58498. The assignee was removed after a long period with no updates; status is stalled.
summaries.40648: Comments confirm the bug persists through 2025 on all tested stable versions. The trailing space is visually trimmed/not rendered when TextAlign.right is used. A Skia bug was filed at bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=11933 and accepted. One commenter notes SkParagraph treats \r as a soft line break with CodeUnitFlag 0x0006, which may be related. A layout workaround using IntrinsicWidth inside a Row was suggested. No fix has landed in Flutter; status is stalled.
summaries.167411: Comments confirm the issue: when a Flutter app is embedded in a div on a web page that itself scrolls, wheel/trackpad scroll events over enabled TextField widgets propagate out of the Flutter container and scroll the host page. Disabling the TextField stops the propagation. Confirmed reproducible on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.x). Related issues #159680 and #159358 and engine PR #53922 are linked. No fix has landed; status is open.
summaries.121376: Root cause confirmed in comments: the Windows (and Linux) IME keeps an internal composition buffer; calling TextEditingController.clear() without first breaking the composition (unfocus/refocus) leaves stale buffer state that injects spurious characters on subsequent input. The issue reproduces on Windows and Chrome-on-Windows but not macOS or Chrome-on-macOS. The workaround is to call focusNode.unfocus() before clearing, then requestFocus() in a post-frame callback. A later comment confirms the bug also affects Linux. Status is open with a known workaround.
summaries.117642: Comments confirm the issue: with TextInputType.multiline and TextInputAction.send on iOS, the Sogou (and similar Chinese third-party) keyboard's line-feed key inserts a space instead of a newline. A commenter traced the root cause to Flutter's engine detecting newlines via monitoring for \n in input, but the Sogou keyboard inserts two carriage returns (\r) instead; SkParagraph treats \r as a soft line break. A workaround FilteringTextInputFormatter was proposed to replace \r with \n. Status is open.
summaries.143997: Root cause identified in comments: Windows 11's clipboard history popup synthesizes Ctrl+V key events using a non-standard physical key ID (0x1600000000 instead of the real Control Left scan code), which the Windows embedder does not correctly map to standard Flutter key events, so the paste shortcut is never triggered. Confirmed Windows 11-specific; works on Windows 10 and web (though web throws an assertion error). A community workaround that intercepts PlatformDispatcher.onKeyData and remaps the synthetic key codes was shared. The fix requires changes in the Windows embedder (engine-side). Status is open and stalled.
